checkpoint_role: same checkpoint on both H2H sides; packing measurement only
recommendation: Use unbound 16 workers/GPU for the next full-game H100 packing canary. Do not add GPU-local CPU affinity: it regressed this B200 workload. Retain the sealed production default until the H100 canary confirms end-to-end games/hour and zero errors.
limitations: Every game intentionally stopped at max_decisions=60, so this is a short-horizon packing microbenchmark, not full-game throughput proof.

The evaluator derives search RNG seeds from worker index; changing worker count therefore changes a small amount of explored work. Simulations/second is reported alongside equal game count to expose that variation.
This B200 result does not prove the H100 optimum. H100 full-game validation is required before changing the production worker default.
The unbound 8/16 controls used the same seeds and exact respective simulation counts as their bound counterparts, making the observed CPU-affinity regression directly comparable on this host.
No strength conclusion may be drawn because both roles used identical checkpoint bytes.

reproduction.serialization: worker counts ran sequentially in the order bound 8,16,24 then unbound 16,8
reproduction.command_shape: taskset -c 0-25 python tools/gumbel_search_cross_net_h2h.py --candidate CHECKPOINT --baseline CHECKPOINT --pairs 24 --base-seed 6199900100 --workers {8|16|24} --threads-per-worker 1 --device cuda --n-full 16 --max-decisions 60 --lazy-interior-chance --correct-rust-chance-spectra --public-observation --information-set-search --determinization-particles 4 --determinization-min-simulations 32 --symmetry-averaged-eval --symmetry-averaged-eval-threshold 20 --c-scale 0.03 --gate-config flywheel --out REPORT
